maml:description.maml:para: Compresses and optionally encrypts items in persistent storage.

Compresses stored items using GZip compression to reduce storage size. Optionally encrypts compressed data using AES-256 encryption with PBKDF2 key derivation (100,000 iterations, SHA-256). The compressed item is saved with `.gz` extension (or `.gz.enc` if encrypted). Supports in-place compression or creating a new compressed copy. Ideal for archiving large datasets or reducing storage costs with security.
Compression level: Optimal (default), Fastest, or NoCompression.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Destination name for compressed item. If not specified, adds `.gz` extension (or `.gz.enc` if encrypted).
If specified, overwrites existing compressed item without confirmation.
If specified, keeps the original item after compression.
The name/key of the item to compress.
If specified, returns compression statistics (SourceName, DestinationName, OriginalSize, CompressedSize, FinalSize, CompressionRatio, IsEncrypted).
Password for AES-256 encryption. If specified, compressed data will be encrypted. Use `Read-Host -AsSecureString` to securely prompt for password.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Compression level: Optimal (default), Fastest, or NoCompression.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Destination name for compressed item. If not specified, adds `.gz` extension (or `.gz.enc` if encrypted).
If specified, overwrites existing compressed item without confirmation.
If specified, keeps the original item after compression.
The name/key of the item to compress.
If specified, returns compression statistics (SourceName, DestinationName, OriginalSize, CompressedSize, FinalSize, CompressionRatio, IsEncrypted).
Password for AES-256 encryption. If specified, compressed data will be encrypted. Use `Read-Host -AsSecureString` to securely prompt for password.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Item name to compress.
When `-PassThru` is specified, returns an object with compression statistics.

maml:alert.maml:para: Encryption uses AES-256-CBC with PBKDF2 key derivation (100,000 iterations, SHA-256, 32-byte salt). Binary format: `[Magic Header 11B][Salt 32B][IV 16B][Encrypted Data]`. Use `Expand-PSDataRepositoryItem` with the same password to decrypt.

maml:introduction.maml:para: Compresses largefile.json to largefile.json.gz and removes the original.
Compresses and encrypts sensitive.json to sensitive.json.gz.enc.
Compresses all log files, keeping originals.
Compresses data.json to a specific destination path.

maml:description.maml:para: Connects to a data repository (Azure Blob, Queue, Service Bus, Key Vault, Disk, or InMemory).
Establishes a session to a data repository provider. The provider type is automatically detected from the supplied parameters (connection string, account name, vault name, etc.).
Supports 12 authentication methods including connection strings, SAS tokens, Managed Identity, client secrets/certificates, interactive browser, device code, and DefaultAzureCredential.
For local development, use `-Disk` or `-InMemory` switches which require no authentication.
The provider is auto-detected based on supplied parameters:
- `-VaultName` → AzureKeyVault
- `-TopicName` / `-SubscriptionName` → AzureServiceBus
- `-QueueName` → AzureQueue
- `-ContainerName` → AzureBlob
- `-Disk` → Disk
- `-InMemory` → InMemory
Storage account name, namespace, or path.
Container, queue, or topic name.
Use Disk provider with file-based storage (no authentication required).
Maximum number of retry attempts for failed operations.
Maximum delay in seconds between retry attempts (exponential backoff cap).
File path for Disk provider storage.
Proxy URI for corporate proxy (e.g., `http://proxy.corp.local:8080`).
Queue name (for queue providers).
Delay in seconds between retry attempts.
Subscription name (for Service Bus topics).
Test connection after establishing it to validate connectivity.
Connection timeout in seconds.
Topic name (for Service Bus topics).
Key Vault name (for Azure Key Vault provider).
Storage account name, namespace, or path.
Container, queue, or topic name.
Use InMemory provider for volatile storage (no authentication required).
Maximum number of retry attempts for failed operations.
Maximum delay in seconds between retry attempts (exponential backoff cap).
Proxy URI for corporate proxy (e.g., `http://proxy.corp.local:8080`).
Queue name (for queue providers).
Delay in seconds between retry attempts.
Subscription name (for Service Bus topics).
Test connection after establishing it to validate connectivity.
Connection timeout in seconds.
Topic name (for Service Bus topics).
Key Vault name (for Azure Key Vault provider).
Storage account name, namespace, or path.
Connection string with AccountKey or SharedAccessKey.
Container, queue, or topic name.
Maximum number of retry attempts for failed operations.
Maximum delay in seconds between retry attempts (exponential backoff cap).
Proxy URI for corporate proxy (e.g., `http://proxy.corp.local:8080`).
Queue name (for queue providers).
Delay in seconds between retry attempts.
Subscription name (for Service Bus topics).
Test connection after establishing it to validate connectivity.
Connection timeout in seconds.
Topic name (for Service Bus topics).
Key Vault name (for Azure Key Vault provider).
Storage account name, namespace, or path.
Container, queue, or topic name.
Maximum number of retry attempts for failed operations.
Maximum delay in seconds between retry attempts (exponential backoff cap).
Proxy URI for corporate proxy (e.g., `http://proxy.corp.local:8080`).
Queue name (for queue providers).
Delay in seconds between retry attempts.
SAS (Shared Access Signature) token or full SAS URI.
Subscription name (for Service Bus topics).
Test connection after establishing it to validate connectivity.
Connection timeout in seconds.
Topic name (for Service Bus topics).
Key Vault name (for Azure Key Vault provider).
Storage account name, namespace, or path.
Azure AD Application (Client) ID.
Azure AD Application Client Secret.
Container, queue, or topic name.
Maximum number of retry attempts for failed operations.
Maximum delay in seconds between retry attempts (exponential backoff cap).
Proxy URI for corporate proxy (e.g., `http://proxy.corp.local:8080`).
Queue name (for queue providers).
Delay in seconds between retry attempts.
Subscription name (for Service Bus topics).
Azure AD Tenant ID.
Test connection after establishing it to validate connectivity.
Connection timeout in seconds.
Topic name (for Service Bus topics).
Key Vault name (for Azure Key Vault provider).
Storage account name, namespace, or path.
Certificate password.
Path to certificate file (.pfx).
Azure AD Application (Client) ID.
Container, queue, or topic name.
Maximum number of retry attempts for failed operations.
Maximum delay in seconds between retry attempts (exponential backoff cap).
Proxy URI for corporate proxy (e.g., `http://proxy.corp.local:8080`).
Queue name (for queue providers).
Delay in seconds between retry attempts.
Subscription name (for Service Bus topics).
Azure AD Tenant ID.
Test connection after establishing it to validate connectivity.
Connection timeout in seconds.
Topic name (for Service Bus topics).
Key Vault name (for Azure Key Vault provider).
Storage account name, namespace, or path.
Azure AD Application (Client) ID.
Container, queue, or topic name.
Maximum number of retry attempts for failed operations.
Maximum delay in seconds between retry attempts (exponential backoff cap).
Proxy URI for corporate proxy (e.g., `http://proxy.corp.local:8080`).
Queue name (for queue providers).
Delay in seconds between retry attempts.
Subscription name (for Service Bus topics).
Azure AD Tenant ID.
Test connection after establishing it to validate connectivity.
Connection timeout in seconds.
Token file path for Workload Identity.
Topic name (for Service Bus topics).
Key Vault name (for Azure Key Vault provider).
Use Workload Identity (Kubernetes) for authentication.
Storage account name, namespace, or path.
Azure AD Application (Client) ID.
Container, queue, or topic name.
Use interactive browser authentication.
Maximum number of retry attempts for failed operations.
Maximum delay in seconds between retry attempts (exponential backoff cap).
Proxy URI for corporate proxy (e.g., `http://proxy.corp.local:8080`).
Queue name (for queue providers).
Delay in seconds between retry attempts.
Subscription name (for Service Bus topics).
Azure AD Tenant ID.
Test connection after establishing it to validate connectivity.
Connection timeout in seconds.
Topic name (for Service Bus topics).
Key Vault name (for Azure Key Vault provider).
Storage account name, namespace, or path.
Azure AD Application (Client) ID.
Container, queue, or topic name.
Use device code authentication.
Maximum number of retry attempts for failed operations.
Maximum delay in seconds between retry attempts (exponential backoff cap).
Proxy URI for corporate proxy (e.g., `http://proxy.corp.local:8080`).
Queue name (for queue providers).
Delay in seconds between retry attempts.
Subscription name (for Service Bus topics).
Azure AD Tenant ID.
Test connection after establishing it to validate connectivity.
Connection timeout in seconds.
Topic name (for Service Bus topics).
Key Vault name (for Azure Key Vault provider).
Storage account name, namespace, or path.
Container, queue, or topic name.
Maximum number of retry attempts for failed operations.
Maximum delay in seconds between retry attempts (exponential backoff cap).
Proxy URI for corporate proxy (e.g., `http://proxy.corp.local:8080`).
Queue name (for queue providers).
Delay in seconds between retry attempts.
Use shared token cache authentication.
Subscription name (for Service Bus topics).
Azure AD Tenant ID.
Test connection after establishing it to validate connectivity.
Connection timeout in seconds.
Topic name (for Service Bus topics).
Username for shared token cache.
Key Vault name (for Azure Key Vault provider).
Storage account name, namespace, or path.
Container, queue, or topic name.
Use DefaultAzureCredential (tries multiple authentication methods automatically).
Maximum number of retry attempts for failed operations.
Maximum delay in seconds between retry attempts (exponential backoff cap).
Proxy URI for corporate proxy (e.g., `http://proxy.corp.local:8080`).
Queue name (for queue providers).
Delay in seconds between retry attempts.
Subscription name (for Service Bus topics).
Azure AD Tenant ID.
Test connection after establishing it to validate connectivity.
Connection timeout in seconds.
Topic name (for Service Bus topics).
Key Vault name (for Azure Key Vault provider).
Storage account name, namespace, or path.
Azure AD Application (Client) ID.
Container, queue, or topic name.
Use Managed Identity for authentication.
Maximum number of retry attempts for failed operations.
Maximum delay in seconds between retry attempts (exponential backoff cap).
Proxy URI for corporate proxy (e.g., `http://proxy.corp.local:8080`).
Queue name (for queue providers).
Delay in seconds between retry attempts.
Subscription name (for Service Bus topics).
Test connection after establishing it to validate connectivity.
Connection timeout in seconds.
Topic name (for Service Bus topics).
Key Vault name (for Azure Key Vault provider).
Storage account name, namespace, or path.
Certificate password.
Path to certificate file (.pfx).
Azure AD Application (Client) ID.
Azure AD Application Client Secret.
Connection string with AccountKey or SharedAccessKey.
Container, queue, or topic name.
Use DefaultAzureCredential (tries multiple authentication methods automatically).
Use device code authentication.
Use Disk provider with file-based storage (no authentication required).
Use InMemory provider for volatile storage (no authentication required).
Use interactive browser authentication.
Use Managed Identity for authentication.
Maximum number of retry attempts for failed operations.
Maximum delay in seconds between retry attempts (exponential backoff cap).
File path for Disk provider storage.
Proxy URI for corporate proxy (e.g., `http://proxy.corp.local:8080`).
Queue name (for queue providers).
Delay in seconds between retry attempts.
SAS (Shared Access Signature) token or full SAS URI.
Use shared token cache authentication.
Subscription name (for Service Bus topics).
Azure AD Tenant ID.
Test connection after establishing it to validate connectivity.
Connection timeout in seconds.
Token file path for Workload Identity.
Topic name (for Service Bus topics).
Username for shared token cache.
Key Vault name (for Azure Key Vault provider).
Use Workload Identity (Kubernetes) for authentication.
Connection confirmation message.

maml:alert.maml:para:
The provider type is auto-detected from supplied parameters. Connection strings and sensitive values are sanitized in verbose output.
When `-TestConnection` is specified and the test fails, the session is automatically cleared.
Enterprise features (proxy, retries, timeout) are only applied to Azure providers, not Disk/InMemory.

maml:introduction.maml:para: Connects to a local disk-based repository at the specified path.
Connects to a volatile in-memory repository (useful for testing).
Connects to Azure Blob Storage using a connection string.
Connects to Azure Queue Storage using DefaultAzureCredential.
Connects to Azure Key Vault using Managed Identity.
Connects to an Azure Service Bus topic with a subscription.
Connects via proxy and validates the connection after establishing it.

maml:description.maml:para: Deserializes a raw message string into a PowerShell object.

Converts a raw message string (e.g. received from an Azure Function Queue trigger) into a deserialized object using the module's Formatter. Does not require an active session or queue connection. Automatically detects the serialization format (JSON, XML, CSV) unless `-Format` is specified. If the message was sent with `-IncludeMetadata`, the metadata envelope is automatically unwrapped and the inner content is deserialized. Use `-IncludeMetadata` to attach the envelope metadata as note properties on the output object.
Deserialization format. If not specified, attempts auto-detection based on content.

If specified, includes metadata properties from the envelope (TypeName, Timestamp, MachineName, UserName, SerializationFormat) as note properties on the deserialized object. Only applies when the message contains a metadata envelope (sent with `-IncludeMetadata` on `Send-PSDataRepositoryMessage`).
The raw message string to deserialize. Accepts pipeline input.
Deserialization format. If not specified, attempts auto-detection based on content.

If specified, includes metadata properties from the envelope (TypeName, Timestamp, MachineName, UserName, SerializationFormat) as note properties on the deserialized object. Only applies when the message contains a metadata envelope (sent with `-IncludeMetadata` on `Send-PSDataRepositoryMessage`).
The raw message string to deserialize. Accepts pipeline input.
Raw message string (from Azure Function trigger, Service Bus, etc.).
Deserialized object. When `-IncludeMetadata` is used, additional note properties are added: ` _TypeName`, ` SerializationFormat`, ` Timestamp`, ` MachineName`, ` _UserName`.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet does not require an active session. It is designed for use in Azure Functions, Logic Apps, or any scenario where raw message strings need to be deserialized outside of a PSDataRepository session.

maml:introduction.maml:para: Deserializes a message received from an Azure Function Queue trigger.
Explicitly deserializes a JSON message from pipeline input.
Deserializes a message and includes envelope metadata (TypeName, Timestamp, etc.) as note properties.

maml:description.maml:para: Disconnects from the current data repository session.

Closes the active repository connection established by `Connect-PSDataRepository`. Disposes all underlying resources (storage clients, queue clients, cached connections) and clears the session state. Returns `$true` on success, `$false` if an error occurred during cleanup. Safe to call even when no session is active.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.
Returns `$true` on successful disconnect, `$false` if cleanup encountered an error.

maml:alert.maml:para: Safe to call multiple times or when no session is active.

maml:introduction.maml:para: Disconnects from the current repository and releases all resources.

maml:description.maml:para: Decompresses and optionally decrypts items in persistent storage.

Decompresses GZip-compressed items stored in the repository. Automatically decrypts items encrypted with `Compress-PSDataRepositoryItem` if password is provided. Automatically detects `.gz` and `.gz.enc` extensions and removes them from decompressed item name. Supports in-place decompression or creating a new decompressed copy. Ideal for restoring archived datasets or processing compressed/encrypted data.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Destination name for decompressed item. If not specified, removes `.gz` or `.gz.enc` extension from original name.
If specified, overwrites existing decompressed item without confirmation.
If specified, keeps the compressed item after decompression.
The name/key of the compressed item to expand.
If specified, returns decompression statistics (SourceName, DestinationName, OriginalSize, CompressedSize, DecompressedSize, ExpansionRatio, WasEncrypted).
Password for AES-256 decryption. Required if item was encrypted with `Compress-PSDataRepositoryItem`. Use `Read-Host -AsSecureString` to securely prompt for password.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Destination name for decompressed item. If not specified, removes `.gz` or `.gz.enc` extension from original name.
If specified, overwrites existing decompressed item without confirmation.
If specified, keeps the compressed item after decompression.
The name/key of the compressed item to expand.
If specified, returns decompression statistics (SourceName, DestinationName, OriginalSize, CompressedSize, DecompressedSize, ExpansionRatio, WasEncrypted).
Password for AES-256 decryption. Required if item was encrypted with `Compress-PSDataRepositoryItem`. Use `Read-Host -AsSecureString` to securely prompt for password.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Compressed item name to expand.
When `-PassThru` is specified, returns an object with decompression statistics.

maml:alert.maml:para: Automatically detects encryption by checking for the magic header written by `Compress-PSDataRepositoryItem`. If the file is encrypted but no `-Password` is provided, the cmdlet writes an error. If `-Password` is provided but the file is not encrypted, a warning is shown.

maml:introduction.maml:para: Decompresses largefile.json.gz to largefile.json and removes compressed file.
Decrypts and decompresses encrypted file with password.
Decompresses all .gz files, keeping compressed originals.

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves items from persistent storage (Blob, Disk).

Loads and deserializes items from the connected repository (Azure Blob Storage, Disk). Supports retrieving single items, multiple items by pattern, or listing all items. Items are automatically deserialized based on format (JSON, XML, CSV) or content detection. Ideal for loading configuration, datasets, or processing results.
If specified, returns content as byte array (binary mode).
If specified, continues processing even if individual object retrieval fails.
Text encoding for reading. Default: UTF-8.
Expected deserialization format. If not specified, auto-detects from content or file extension.
If specified, includes metadata (Name, Path, Format) as properties on output objects (` _Name`, ` Path`, ` _Format`).
The name/key or pattern of the object(s) to retrieve. Supports wildcards (* and ?) depending on provider.
If specified, returns raw content as string without deserialization.
If specified, returns content as byte array (binary mode).
If specified, continues processing even if individual object retrieval fails.
Text encoding for reading. Default: UTF-8.
Expected deserialization format. If not specified, auto-detects from content or file extension.
If specified, includes metadata (Name, Path, Format) as properties on output objects (` _Name`, ` Path`, ` _Format`).
If specified, lists all available objects in the repository.
If specified, returns raw content as string without deserialization.
If specified, returns content as byte array (binary mode).
If specified, continues processing even if individual object retrieval fails.
Text encoding for reading. Default: UTF-8.
Expected deserialization format. If not specified, auto-detects from content or file extension.
If specified, includes metadata (Name, Path, Format) as properties on output objects (` _Name`, ` Path`, ` _Format`).
If specified, lists all available objects in the repository.
The name/key or pattern of the object(s) to retrieve. Supports wildcards (* and ?) depending on provider.
If specified, returns raw content as string without deserialization.
Item names can be piped to this cmdlet.
{{ Fill in the Description }}
Deserialized object(s). The output type depends on the stored content and format.

maml:alert.maml:para: Format auto-detection uses file extension first, then content analysis (JSON: `{`/`[`, XML: `<`, CSV: header row).

maml:introduction.maml:para: Retrieves all JSON files from the data folder.

maml:description.maml:para: Lists all supported PSDataRepository providers and their capabilities.

Displays information about each data repository provider the module can work with, including provider type, supported capabilities (Storage, Queue, Secrets), authentication methods, and connection examples. Does not require an active session.
Filter providers by capability.
Filter by provider name. If not specified, all providers are listed.
Filter providers by capability.
Filter by provider name. If not specified, all providers are listed.

One object per provider with properties:
| Property | Type | Description | |---|---|---| | Name | String | Provider name | | Description | String | Human-readable description | | Capabilities | String[] | Storage, Queue, and/or Secrets | | AuthMethods | String[] | Supported authentication methods | | RequiresAzure | Boolean | Whether Azure subscription is needed | | Example | String | Example connection command |

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet does not require an active session.

maml:introduction.maml:para: Lists all available providers with their capabilities.
Shows details for the AzureBlob provider.
Lists only providers that support queue messaging.

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves a secret value from the connected repository.
Gets the value of a secret from the connected repository (typically Azure Key Vault or FileSystem). Fails if the repository type does not support secrets or is not connected.
The name of the secret to retrieve.
The name of the secret to retrieve.
{{ Fill in the Description }}
The secret value as a plain text string.
{{ Fill in the Description }}

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires an active session to a provider that supports secrets (e.g., AzureKeyVault).

maml:introduction.maml:para: Retrieves the value of the secret named "ApiKey".

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves information about the current repository session.
Displays details about the active PSDataRepository session including:
- Connection status
- Provider type (AzureBlob, AzureQueue, Disk, InMemory, etc.)
- Authentication mode
- Repository identity/endpoint
- Capabilities (Storage, Queue, Secrets, Filesystem)
Useful for debugging connectivity issues or verifying session configuration.

A custom object with the following properties:
| Property | Type | Description | |---|---|---| | IsConnected | Boolean | Whether a session is active | | Provider | String | Provider name (AzureBlob, Disk, etc.) | | RepositoryType | String | Internal repository class name | | ConnectionType | String | Cloud, FileSystem, Memory, or FluentStorage | | AuthenticationMode | String | Authentication method used | | RepositoryIdentity | String | Account name, path, or sanitized endpoint | | Namespace | String | Container/queue/topic name (if applicable) | | TopicName | String | Service Bus topic (if applicable) | | SubscriptionName | String | Service Bus subscription (if applicable) | | Capabilities | String | Comma-separated list: Storage, Queue, Secrets, Filesystem |

maml:alert.maml:para: Connection strings, account keys, and SAS tokens are automatically sanitized in the output.

maml:introduction.maml:para: Shows detailed session information in list format.

maml:description.maml:para: Receives and deserializes messages from the connected queue repository.

Retrieves messages from the active queue repository and deserializes them back to objects. Supports batching for efficient retrieval of large message volumes. Can operate in single-message mode, batch mode, continuous polling mode, or by-ID peek mode. Messages are automatically acknowledged/deleted after successful deserialization by default.
If specified, continues processing even if individual message deserialization fails.
Deserialization format. If not specified, attempts auto-detection based on content.

If specified, includes message metadata (Id, Receipt, DequeueCount, timestamps) as note properties on output objects: ` _MessageId`, ` MessageReceipt`, ` DequeueCount`, ` InsertedOn`, ` ExpiresOn`, ` _NextVisibleOn`.
Maximum number of messages to receive in one call.
If specified, does not automatically delete/acknowledge messages after processing. Useful for manual acknowledgement scenarios or debugging.
If specified, returns raw message content without deserialization.
Visibility timeout for received messages (how long they remain invisible to other consumers).
If specified, continues processing even if individual message deserialization fails.
Deserialization format. If not specified, attempts auto-detection based on content.

If specified, includes message metadata (Id, Receipt, DequeueCount, timestamps) as note properties on output objects: ` _MessageId`, ` MessageReceipt`, ` DequeueCount`, ` InsertedOn`, ` ExpiresOn`, ` _NextVisibleOn`.
Maximum number of messages to receive in one call.
If specified, does not automatically delete/acknowledge messages after processing. Useful for manual acknowledgement scenarios or debugging.
If specified, peeks messages without removing them from the queue (read-only).
If specified, returns raw message content without deserialization.
Visibility timeout for received messages (how long they remain invisible to other consumers).
If specified, continues processing even if individual message deserialization fails.
If specified, continues polling continuously until interrupted (Ctrl+C) or `-MaxIterations` is reached.
Delay in seconds between continuous polling attempts. Only used with `-Continuous`.
Deserialization format. If not specified, attempts auto-detection based on content.

If specified, includes message metadata (Id, Receipt, DequeueCount, timestamps) as note properties on output objects: ` _MessageId`, ` MessageReceipt`, ` DequeueCount`, ` InsertedOn`, ` ExpiresOn`, ` _NextVisibleOn`.
Maximum number of continuous polling iterations. Only used with `-Continuous`.
Maximum number of messages to receive in one call.
If specified, does not automatically delete/acknowledge messages after processing. Useful for manual acknowledgement scenarios or debugging.
If specified, returns raw message content without deserialization.
Visibility timeout for received messages (how long they remain invisible to other consumers).
If specified, continues processing even if individual message deserialization fails.
Deserialization format. If not specified, attempts auto-detection based on content.

If specified, includes message metadata (Id, Receipt, DequeueCount, timestamps) as note properties on output objects: ` _MessageId`, ` MessageReceipt`, ` DequeueCount`, ` InsertedOn`, ` ExpiresOn`, ` _NextVisibleOn`.
Maximum number of messages to receive in one call.
The ID of a specific message to retrieve. When specified, only the message with the matching ID is returned (using peek, without removing from queue).
If specified, does not automatically delete/acknowledge messages after processing. Useful for manual acknowledgement scenarios or debugging.
If specified, returns raw message content without deserialization.
Visibility timeout for received messages (how long they remain invisible to other consumers).
If specified, continues processing even if individual message deserialization fails.
If specified, continues polling continuously until interrupted (Ctrl+C) or `-MaxIterations` is reached.
Delay in seconds between continuous polling attempts. Only used with `-Continuous`.
Deserialization format. If not specified, attempts auto-detection based on content.

If specified, includes message metadata (Id, Receipt, DequeueCount, timestamps) as note properties on output objects: ` _MessageId`, ` MessageReceipt`, ` DequeueCount`, ` InsertedOn`, ` ExpiresOn`, ` _NextVisibleOn`.
Maximum number of continuous polling iterations. Only used with `-Continuous`.
Maximum number of messages to receive in one call.
The ID of a specific message to retrieve. When specified, only the message with the matching ID is returned (using peek, without removing from queue).
If specified, does not automatically delete/acknowledge messages after processing. Useful for manual acknowledgement scenarios or debugging.
If specified, peeks messages without removing them from the queue (read-only).
If specified, returns raw message content without deserialization.
Visibility timeout for received messages (how long they remain invisible to other consumers).
Deserialized message objects. When `-IncludeMetadata` is used, additional note properties are added.

maml:alert.maml:para: In the default (non-Peek) mode, messages are automatically deleted after successful processing. Use `-NoAutoDelete` to disable this behavior and manually delete with `Remove-PSDataRepositoryMessage`.

maml:introduction.maml:para: Receives up to 10 messages and returns them as deserialized objects.
Continuously polls for messages, retrieving up to 100 per batch with 5-second delay between polls. Press Ctrl+C to stop.
Peeks at messages without removing them from the queue.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes items from persistent storage (Blob, Disk).
Deletes items from the connected repository (Azure Blob Storage, Disk). Supports removing single or multiple items by name. Prompts for confirmation before deleting unless `-Force` is specified.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
If specified, continues processing even if individual object deletion fails.
If specified, skips confirmation prompts.
The name/key of the item(s) to remove.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
If specified, continues processing even if individual object deletion fails.
If specified, skips confirmation prompts.
The name/key of the item(s) to remove.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Item names can be piped to this cmdlet.
{{ Fill in the Description }}

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires an active session to a storage provider. Uses `ConfirmImpact = High`.

maml:introduction.maml:para: Removes temp.json from storage (with confirmation prompt).
Removes all JSON files in backup folder without confirmation.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes/deletes messages from the queue.

Deletes messages from the connected queue repository (InMemory, Disk, Azure Queue, Service Bus). Typically used after successful message processing to prevent reprocessing. Messages must have been received first (to obtain receipt handles). For Azure providers, uses receipt handle for deletion. For Disk/InMemory, uses message ID.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
If specified, suppresses confirmation prompts.
The message object(s) to delete. Typically obtained from `Receive-PSDataRepositoryMessage`.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
If specified, suppresses confirmation prompts.
Pipeline object from Receive-PSDataRepositoryMessage.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
If specified, suppresses confirmation prompts.
Pipeline object from Receive-PSDataRepositoryMessage.
The message object(s) to delete. Typically obtained from `Receive-PSDataRepositoryMessage`.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Message objects from `Receive-PSDataRepositoryMessage`.
{{ Fill in the Description }}
{{ Fill in the Description }}

maml:alert.maml:para: If using `Receive-PSDataRepositoryMessage` without `-NoAutoDelete`, messages are automatically deleted after successful processing. This cmdlet is needed only when `-NoAutoDelete` is used.

maml:introduction.maml:para: Receives and then deletes a message.
Receives and deletes multiple messages via pipeline.

maml:description.maml:para: Deletes a secret from the connected repository.
Removes (deletes) a secret from the connected repository (typically Azure Key Vault or FileSystem). Fails if the repository type does not support secrets or is not connected.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Skip confirmation prompts.
The name of the secret to remove.
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Skip confirmation prompts.
The name of the secret to remove.
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.
{{ Fill in the Description }}

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires an active session to a secrets provider (e.g., AzureKeyVault).

maml:introduction.maml:para: Deletes the secret named "ApiKey" from the connected repository.

maml:description.maml:para: Sends objects to the connected queue repository.

Serializes and sends objects to the active queue repository (InMemory, Disk, Azure Queue, Service Bus). Supports pipeline input for processing large volumes of objects efficiently. Objects are serialized to JSON by default, with configurable format (XML, CSV). Uses batching for optimal performance (default batch size: 100 messages).
On pipeline interruption (Ctrl+C), any buffered messages are flushed before stopping.
Batch size for sending messages. Larger batches improve throughput but use more memory.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
If specified, continues processing even if individual message serialization fails.
CSV delimiter character (only for CSV format).
Skip confirmation prompts.
Serialization format: Json (default), Xml, or Csv.
Include machine/user info in metadata (security-sensitive).
If specified, includes metadata envelope with type information, timestamp, machine name, and username. The envelope can be unwrapped by `ConvertFrom-PSDataRepositoryMessage -IncludeMetadata`.
The object(s) to send. Accepts pipeline input.
Maximum serialization depth to prevent infinite recursion.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
XML root element name (only for XML format). Leave empty for auto-detection from object type.
Batch size for sending messages. Larger batches improve throughput but use more memory.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
If specified, continues processing even if individual message serialization fails.
CSV delimiter character (only for CSV format).
Skip confirmation prompts.
Serialization format: Json (default), Xml, or Csv.
Include machine/user info in metadata (security-sensitive).
If specified, includes metadata envelope with type information, timestamp, machine name, and username. The envelope can be unwrapped by `ConvertFrom-PSDataRepositoryMessage -IncludeMetadata`.
The object(s) to send. Accepts pipeline input.
Maximum serialization depth to prevent infinite recursion.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
XML root element name (only for XML format). Leave empty for auto-detection from object type.
Any PowerShell object. Accepts pipeline input.
Summary message with count of messages sent.

maml:alert.maml:para: Messages are buffered and sent in batches for performance. The batch is flushed at the end of pipeline or when buffer reaches `-BatchSize`.

maml:introduction.maml:para: $tasks = @([PSCustomObject]@{ Id = 1; Action = "Backup"; Target = "DB01" }; [PSCustomObject]@{ Id = 2; Action = "Restart"; Target = "App02" }) $tasks | Send-PSDataRepositoryMessage
Sends a collection of objects to the queue (serialized as JSON).
Sends 100,000 custom objects with batching of 500 messages.

maml:description.maml:para: Saves items to persistent storage (Blob, Disk).

Serializes and stores items in the connected repository (Azure Blob Storage, Disk). Supports pipeline input for batch processing and multiple serialization formats. Items are stored as individual files/blobs with specified names. Ideal for saving configuration, datasets, or processing results.

When receiving pipeline input with a fixed name (no `{0}` placeholder), automatically accumulates all objects and saves them as a single collection (array). Use `{0}` in `-Name` for per-object naming, or `-NameProperty` for property-based naming.
If specified, accumulates all pipeline objects and saves them as a collection (array). Useful for creating a single JSON/XML/CSV file from multiple pipeline objects.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
CSV delimiter character. Only used when Format = Csv. Default: comma (`,`).
Text encoding for writing. Default: UTF-8.
If specified, overwrites existing objects without confirmation.
Serialization format: Json (default), Xml, or Csv.
The object to save. Accepts pipeline input.
Maximum serialization depth. Default: 10.
The name/key for storing the object. Can include `{0}` placeholder for pipeline index. Examples: `"data.json"`, `"item-{0}.json"`, `"backup/{0}.xml"`
If specified, returns storage path/URI after saving.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
XML root element name. Only used when Format = Xml. If not specified, automatically inferred from object type.
If specified, accumulates all pipeline objects and saves them as a collection (array). Useful for creating a single JSON/XML/CSV file from multiple pipeline objects.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
CSV delimiter character. Only used when Format = Csv. Default: comma (`,`).
Text encoding for writing. Default: UTF-8.
If specified, overwrites existing objects without confirmation.
Serialization format: Json (default), Xml, or Csv.
The object to save. Accepts pipeline input.
Maximum serialization depth. Default: 10.
Prefix to add before generated names (used with `-NameProperty`).
Property name to use for generating unique names from pipeline objects. Example: `-NameProperty "Id"` uses each object's Id property as the name.
Suffix/extension to add after generated names (used with `-NameProperty`).
If specified, returns storage path/URI after saving.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
XML root element name. Only used when Format = Xml. If not specified, automatically inferred from object type.
If specified, accumulates all pipeline objects and saves them as a collection (array). Useful for creating a single JSON/XML/CSV file from multiple pipeline objects.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
CSV delimiter character. Only used when Format = Csv. Default: comma (`,`).
Text encoding for writing. Default: UTF-8.
If specified, overwrites existing objects without confirmation.
Serialization format: Json (default), Xml, or Csv.
The object to save. Accepts pipeline input.
Maximum serialization depth. Default: 10.
The name/key for storing the object. Can include `{0}` placeholder for pipeline index. Examples: `"data.json"`, `"item-{0}.json"`, `"backup/{0}.xml"`
Prefix to add before generated names (used with `-NameProperty`).
Property name to use for generating unique names from pipeline objects. Example: `-NameProperty "Id"` uses each object's Id property as the name.
Suffix/extension to add after generated names (used with `-NameProperty`).
If specified, returns storage path/URI after saving.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
XML root element name. Only used when Format = Xml. If not specified, automatically inferred from object type.
Any PowerShell object. Accepts pipeline input.
When `-PassThru` is specified, returns an object with Name, Path, Size, Format (and ObjectCount for accumulated saves).

maml:alert.maml:para: Auto-accumulate mode is activated when Name does not contain `{0}` and `-NameProperty` is not used. In this mode, all pipeline objects are collected and saved as a single array.

maml:introduction.maml:para: $data = @([PSCustomObject]@{ Id = 1; Name = "Server01"; Status = "Running" }; [PSCustomObject]@{ Id = 2; Name = "Server02"; Status = "Stopped" }) $data | Set-PSDataRepositoryItem -Name "servers.json"
Saves a collection of objects as a JSON array to storage (auto-accumulate mode).
Saves each file item with unique name based on FullName property.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates or updates a secret in the connected repository (Key Vault, FileSystem).
Sets (creates or updates) a secret in the connected repository. Supports either plain text or secure string as the secret value. Supported repositories: AzureKeyVault, FileSystem.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Skip confirmation prompts.
The name of the secret to set.
The secure string value to store as the secret. Preferred over `-Value` for sensitive data.
The plain text value to store as the secret. Use `-SecureValue` for sensitive values.
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Skip confirmation prompts.
The name of the secret to set.
The secure string value to store as the secret. Preferred over `-Value` for sensitive data.
The plain text value to store as the secret. Use `-SecureValue` for sensitive values.
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.
{{ Fill in the Description }}

maml:alert.maml:para: Either `-Value` or `-SecureValue` must be provided. Requires an active session to a secrets provider.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Set a secret with plain text ---------
--------- Set a secret with secure string ---------

maml:description.maml:para: Tests the connectivity to the current PSDataRepository provider.

Validates that the current session's connection to the underlying provider is healthy by performing a lightweight probe operation. For storage providers (Azure Blob, Disk), attempts a list operation. For queue providers (Azure Queue, Azure Service Bus, InMemory), queries the message count. Returns $true if the connection is healthy, $false otherwise.
If no active session exists, returns $false without throwing an error.
This cmdlet is useful for detecting expired tokens, network failures, or stale sessions before executing data operations.
$true if the connection is healthy, $false if no session exists or the connectivity test fails.

maml:alert.maml:para: Does not throw terminating errors. Always returns a boolean value. If the connection test fails, a warning message with the error detail is emitted. For storage providers, the test performs a lightweight list operation. For queue providers, the test queries the approximate message count. Use -Verbose to see which provider is being tested and the result.

maml:introduction.maml:para: Returns $true if the current connection is healthy, $false otherwise.
Checks the connection and reconnects if it has expired or failed.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Health check in a script loop ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: Periodically validates the connection in a long-running script.
Shows detailed diagnostic output including the provider name and test result.

maml:description.maml:para: Tests if an object exists in the repository.

Checks whether one or more objects exist in the connected repository (Azure Blob Storage, Disk). Returns `$true` if the item exists, `$false` otherwise. Requires an active session established by `Connect-PSDataRepository`.
The name/key of the object(s) to test for existence.
The name/key of the object(s) to test for existence.
Item names can be piped to this cmdlet.
{{ Fill in the Description }}
`$true` if the item exists, `$false` otherwise. One boolean per input name.

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires an active session. Use `Connect-PSDataRepository` first.

maml:introduction.maml:para: Conditional execution based on item existence.
